\"...\" || trimmed.starts_with('%') {\n119\t                continue;\n120\t            }\n121\t\n122\t            let indent = calculate_indentation(line);\n123\t\n124\t            // Handle scope {\n128\t                    // Indent increased - entering deeper scope\n129\t                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n130\t                        if ctx.is_parent_key() line_num_1index,\n142\t                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n143\t                            );\n144\t                        }\n145\t                    }\n146\t parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n281\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n282\t                                    dup_err.message()\n283\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n284\t                            }\n285\t                        }\n286\t                    }\n287\t                }\n288\t             format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n356\t                            dup_err.message()\n357\t                        ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n358\t                    }\n359\t                }\n360\t            }\n361\t        }\n362\t\n363\t        // Return parse result using serde_yaml for the actual value\n364\t        match serde_yaml::from_str::<serde_yaml::Value>(content) {\n365\t            Ok(value) => {\n366\t                // Note: parse_errors from scope tracking are currently informational\n367\t                // They could be added as warnings in a future enhancement\n368\t                ParseResult::success(value)\n369\t            }\n370\t            Err(err) => {\n371\t                // Add any duplicate key errors we found to the parse error\n372\t                let error_msg = if parse_errors.is_empty() {\n373\t                    err.to_string()\n374\t                } else {\n375\t ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value> {\n384\t        // Convert bytes to string and parse\n385\t        match std::str::from_utf8(content) {\n386\t            Ok(utf8_content) => self.parse_str(utf8_content),\n387\t            Err(err) => ParseResult::failure(ParseError::io(format!(\"Invalid UTF-8: {}\", err))),\n388\t        }\n389\t    }\n390\t\n391\t    fn parse_file(&self, path: &std::path::Path) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value> {\n392\t        // Read file content\n393\t        match std::fs::read_to_string(path) {\n394\t            Ok(content) => self.parse_str(&content),\n395\t            Err(err) => ParseResult::failure(ParseError::io(format!(\"Failed to read file {}: {}\", path.display(), err))),\n396\t        }\n397\t    }\n398\t\n399\t    fn validate_str(&self, content: &str) -> ValidationResult {\n400\t        // Create syntax validator based on parser mode\n401\t        let validator = if self.config.is_strict() {\n402\t            SyntaxValidator::strict()\n403\t        } else {\n404\t            SyntaxValidator::lenient()\n405\t        };\n406\t\n407\t        // Run syntax validation\n408\t        let mut result = validator.validate(content);\n409\t\n410\t        // Run scope-aware duplicate key detection (when duplicates are not allowed)\n411\t        if !self.config.allow_duplicates {\n412\t            let Core parser trait and implementations for YAML parsing\n//!\n//! This module defines the Parser trait and provides functionality for\n//! parsing YAML content from various sources.\n\nuse crate::parsers::yaml::{\n    types::{ParseResult, ValidationResult, ValidationError},\n    error::ParseError,\n    ParserConfig,\n    syntax_validator::SyntaxValidator,\n    syntax_detector::SyntaxDetector,\n    scope::ScopeStack,\n    line_parser::{calculate_indentation},\n    scope::extract_key_context,\n};\n\n/// Trait for YAML parsers\n///\n/// This trait defines the core interface for parsing YAML content.\n/// Implementations can parse from strings, files, or other sources.\npub trait Parser {\n    /// Parse YAML content from a string\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    /// * `content` - The YAML content as a string\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    /// A ParseResult containing the parsed data or an error\n    fn parse_str(&self, content: &str) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value>;\n\n    /// Parse YAML content from a byte slice\n    fn parse_bytes(&self, content: &[u8]) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value>;\n\n    /// Parse YAML content from a file\n    fn parse_file(&self, path: &std::path::Path) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value>;\n\n    /// Validate YAML content without fully parsing it\n    fn validate_str(&self, content: &str) -> ValidationResult;\n\n    /// Validate a YAML file without fully parsing it\n    fn validate_file(&self, path: &std::path::Path) -> ValidationResult;\n\n    /// Get the parser configuration\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    /// A reference to the parser's configuration\n    fn config(&self) -> &ParserConfig;\n\n    /// Set the parser configuration\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    /// * `config` - The new configuration\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    /// The parser with the new configuration\n    fn with_config(self, config: ParserConfig) -> Self\n    where\n        Self: Sized;\n}\n\n/// Basic YAML parser implementation\n///\n/// This is a minimal implementation of the Parser trait that\n/// provides basic YAML parsing functionality with scope-aware key tracking.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct BasicParser {\n    config: ParserConfig,\n    /// Scope stack for tracking keys within their proper scope contexts\n    scope_stack: ScopeStack,\n}\n\nimpl BasicParser {\n    /// Create a new BasicParser with default configuration\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            config: ParserConfig::default(),\n            scope_stack: ScopeStack::new(2), // Standard 2-space YAML indentation\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new BasicParser with the specified configuration\n    pub fn with_config(config: ParserConfig) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            config,\n            scope_stack: ScopeStack::new(2), // Standard 2-space YAML indentation\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new strict parser\n    ///\n    /// A strict parser enables strict mode and disallows duplicate keys.\n    /// Uses the comprehensive strict configuration from ParserConfig.\n    pub fn strict() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            config: ParserConfig::strict(),\n            scope_stack: ScopeStack::new(2), // Standard 2-space YAML indentation\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Detect duplicate keys using scope-aware tracking\n    ///\n    /// This method processes YAML content line-by-line, tracking keys within\n    /// their proper scope contexts. It returns a list of validation errors\n    /// for any duplicate keys found within the same scope.\n    fn detect_duplicate_keys_with_scope(&self, content: &str, scope_stack: &mut ScopeStack) -> Vec<ValidationError> {\n        let mut duplicate_errors = Vec::new();\n\n        for (line_num, line) in content.lines().enumerate() {\n            let line_num_1index = line_num + 1;\n            let trimmed = line.trim();\n\n            // Skip empty lines and comments\n            if trimmed.is_empty() || trimmed.starts_with('#') {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Skip document markers and directives\n            if trimmed == \"---\" || trimmed == \"...\" || trimmed.starts_with('%') {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            let indent = calculate_indentation(line);\n\n            // Handle scope transitions based on indentation changes\n            use std::cmp::Ordering;\n            match indent.cmp(&scope_stack.current_indent()) {\n                Ordering::Greater => {\n                    // Indent increased - entering deeper scope\n                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n                            // Add the parent key to current scope\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                            // Enter new scope for the parent key's nested content\n                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n                                line_num_1index,\n                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n                            );\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                Ordering::Less => {\n                    // Indent decreased - exit to parent scope\n                    scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n\n                    // After exiting, check if this line has a key\n                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n                            // This is a new parent key at this level\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n                                line_num_1index,\n                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n                            );\n                        } else if ctx.is_inline_scalar() {\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                Ordering::Equal => {\n                    // Same scope - check for keys\n                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n                            // This is a sibling parent key at same indent level\n                            // Exit and re-enter scope for the sibling\n                            scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n                                line_num_1index,\n                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n                            );\n                        } else if ctx.is_inline_scalar() {\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n\n            // Handle sequence items with their own scopes\n            if trimmed.starts_with(\"- \") {\n                if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n                    // Enter a sequence scope for this item\n                    scope_stack.enter_sequence_scope(indent, line_num_1index);\n                    // Add the key to the sequence scope\n                    if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                        duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                            format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                            dup_err.message()\n                        ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        duplicate_errors\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Default for BasicParser {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new()\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Parser for BasicParser {\n    fn parse_str(&self, content: &str) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value> {\n        // Create a new scope stack for this parsing operation\n        let mut scope_stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n        let mut parse_errors = Vec::new();\n\n        // Parse line by line with scope-aware key tracking\n        for (line_num, line) in content.lines().enumerate() {\n            let line_num_1index = line_num + 1;\n            let trimmed = line.trim();\n\n            // Skip empty lines and comments for key tracking\n            if trimmed.is_empty() || trimmed.starts_with('#') {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Skip document markers and directives\n            if trimmed == \"---\" || trimmed == \"...\" || trimmed.starts_with('%') {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            let indent = calculate_indentation(line);\n\n            // Handle scope transitions based on indentation changes\n            use std::cmp::Ordering;\n            match indent.cmp(&scope_stack.current_indent()) {\n                Ordering::Greater => {\n                    // Indent increased - entering deeper scope\n                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n                            // Add the parent key to current scope\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                            // Enter new scope for the parent key's nested content\n                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n                                line_num_1index,\n                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n                            );\n                        } else {\n                            // Indent increased but not a parent key - enter anonymous scope\n                            scope_stack.enter_scope(indent, line_num_1index, None);\n                            // Add the inline key to the new scope\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                Ordering::Less => {\n                    // Indent decreased - exit to parent scope\n                    scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n\n                    // After exiting, check if this line has a key\n                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n                            // This is a new parent key at this level\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n                                line_num_1index,\n                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n                            );\n                        } else if ctx.is_inline_scalar() {\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                Ordering::Equal => {\n                    // Same scope - check for keys\n                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n                            // This is a sibling parent key at same indent level\n                            // Exit and re-enter scope for the sibling\n                            scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n                                line_num_1index,\n                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n                            );\n                        } else if ctx.is_inline_scalar() {\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n\n            // Handle sequence items with their own scopes\n            if trimmed.starts_with(\"- \") {\n                if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n                    // Enter a sequence scope for this item\n                    scope_stack.enter_sequence_scope(indent, line_num_1index);\n                    // Add the key to the sequence scope\n                    if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                        parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                            format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                            dup_err.message()\n                        ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Return parse result using serde_yaml for the actual value\n        match serde_yaml::from_str::<serde_yaml::Value>(content) {\n            Ok(value) => {\n                // Note: parse_errors from scope tracking are currently informational\n                // They could be added as warnings in a future enhancement\n                ParseResult::success(value)\n            }\n            Err(err) => {\n                // Add any duplicate key errors we found to the parse error\n                let error_msg = if parse_errors.is_empty() {\n                    err.to_string()\n                } else {\n                    format!(\"{}; duplicate key errors: {}\", err, parse_errors.iter()\n                        .map(|e| e.message.clone()).collect::<Vec<_>>().join(\"; \"))\n                };\n                ParseResult::failure(ParseError::syntax(error_msg))\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn parse_bytes(&self, content: &[u8]) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value> {\n        // Convert bytes to string and parse\n        match std::str::from_utf8(content) {\n            Ok(utf8_content) => self.parse_str(utf8_content),\n            Err(err) => ParseResult::failure(ParseError::io(format!(\"Invalid UTF-8: {}\", err))),\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn parse_file(&self, path: &std::path::Path) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value> {\n        // Read file content\n        match std::fs::read_to_string(path) {\n            Ok(content) => self.parse_str(&content),\n            Err(err) => ParseResult::failure(ParseError::io(format!(\"Failed to read file {}: {}\", path.display(), err))),\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn validate_str(&self, content: &str) -> ValidationResult {\n        // Create syntax validator based on parser mode\n        let validator = if self.config.is_strict() {\n            SyntaxValidator::strict()\n        } else {\n            SyntaxValidator::lenient()\n        };\n\n        // Run syntax validation\n        let mut result = validator.validate(content);\n\n        // Run scope-aware duplicate key detection (when duplicates are not allowed)\n        if !self.config.allow_duplicates {\n            let mut scope_stack = self.scope_stack.clone();\n            let duplicate_errors = self.detect_duplicate_keys_with_scope(content, &mut scope_stack);\n            if !duplicate_errors.is_empty() {\n                result.valid = false;\n                result.errors.extend(duplicate_errors);\n            }\n        }\n\n        // If no errors from basic validation, run enhanced detection\n        if result.is_valid() {\n            let mut detector = SyntaxDetector::new();\n            let detector_result = detector.detect_to_validation_result(content);\n\n            // Merge errors from detector\n            if !detector_result.is_valid() {\n                result.valid = false;\n                result.errors.extend(detector_result.errors);\n            }\n        }\n\n        result\n    }\n\n    fn validate_file(&self, path: &std::path::Path) -> ValidationResult {\n        // Read file content\n        let content = match std::fs::read_to_string(path) {\n            Ok(content) => content,\n            Err(err) => {\n                return ValidationResult {\n                    valid: false,\n                    errors: vec![ValidationError::new(\n                        path.display().to_string(),\n                        format!(\"failed to read file: {}\", err)\n                    )],\n                    warnings: Vec::new(),\n                };\n            }\n        };\n\n        // Validate the content\n        self.validate_str(&content)\n    }\n\n    fn config(&self) -> &ParserConfig {\n        &self.config\n    }\n\n    fn with_config(self, config: ParserConfig) -> Self\n    where\n        Self: Sized,\n    {\n        Self {\n            config,\n            scope_stack: ScopeStack::new(2), // Standard 2-space YAML indentation\n        }\n    }\n}\n","numLines":469,"startLine":1,"totalLines":469}}}

Comprehensive tests for ScopeStack data structure\n//!\n//! This test module verifies that the ScopeStack correctly:\n//! - Tracks keys per-scope with proper isolation\n//! - Handles scope entry and exit on indent changes\n//! - Detects duplicate keys within the same scope\n//! - Allows same key name in different scopes\n\nuse super::*;\nuse crate::parsers::yaml::scope::{Scope, ScopeStack, DuplicateKeyError};\n\n/// Test helper to verify scope state\nfn verify_scope_state(stack: &ScopeStack, expected_depth: usize, expected_path: &str) {\n    assert_eq!(stack.depth(), expected_depth, \"Scope depth should match expected\");\n    assert_eq!(stack.get_scope_path(), expected_path, \"Scope path should match expected\");\n}\n\n/// Test helper to verify key existence\nfn verify_key_exists(stack: &ScopeStack, key: &str, should_exist: bool) {\n    assert_eq!(\n        stack.contains_key(key),\n        should_exist,\n        \"Key '{}' existence check failed\",\n        key\n    );\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod scope_stack_tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_new_creates_root_scope() {\n        let stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        assert_eq!(stack.base_indent(), 2, \"Base indent should be 2\");\n        assert_eq!(stack.depth(), 1, \"Should have root scope only\");\n        assert_eq!(stack.current_indent(), 0, \"Root scope indent should be 0\");\n        assert_eq!(stack.get_scope_path(), \"\", \"Root scope has no path\");\n        assert!(!stack.in_sequence_context(), \"Root should not be in sequence context\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_new_with_different_base_indents() {\n        let stack_2 = ScopeStack::new(2);\n        let stack_4 = ScopeStack::new(4);\n\n        assert_eq!(stack_2.base_indent(), 2);\n        assert_eq!(stack_4.base_indent(), 4);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_set_base_indent() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n        stack.set_base_indent(4);\n\n        assert_eq!(stack.base_indent(), 4);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_enter_scope_creates_new_scope() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n\n        verify_scope_state(&stack, 2, \"services\");\n        assert_eq!(stack.current_indent(), 2);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_enter_nested_scope() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n        stack.enter_scope(4, 2, Some(\"web\".to_string()));\n        stack.enter_scope(6, 3, Some(\"config\".to_string()));\n\n        verify_scope_state(&stack, 4, \"services.web.config\");\n        assert_eq!(stack.current_indent(), 6);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_enter_scope_without_parent_key() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        stack.enter_scope(2, 1, None);\n\n        verify_scope_state(&stack, 2, \"\");\n        assert_eq!(stack.current_indent(), 2);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_enter_scope_reuses_existing_level() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        // Create first scope at level 2\n        stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"web\".to_string()));\n        stack.add_key(\"host\", 2).unwrap();\n\n        // Exit back to root\n        stack.exit_to_scope(0);\n\n        // Create second scope at same level 2\n        stack.enter_scope(2, 5, Some(\"database\".to_string()));\n\n        // Should be a fresh scope with no keys from previous\n        assert!(!stack.contains_key(\"host\"));\n        verify_scope_state(&stack, 2, \"database\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_exit_to_single_scope() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n        stack.enter_scope(4, 2, Some(\"web\".to_string()));\n\n        verify_scope_state(&stack, 3, \"services.web\");\n\n        stack.exit_to_scope(2);\n\n        verify_scope_state(&stack, 2, \"services\");\n        assert_eq!(stack.current_indent(), 2);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_exit_to_multiple_scopes() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"level1\".to_string()));\n        stack.enter_scope(4, 2, Some(\"level2\".to_string()));\n        stack.enter_scope(6, 3, Some(\"level3\".to_string()));\n        stack.enter_scope(8, 4, Some(\"level4\".to_string()));\n\n        assert_eq!(stack.depth(), 5);\n\n        // Exit directly to level1 (skip intermediate levels)\n        stack.exit_to_scope(2);\n\n        verify_scope_state(&stack, 2, \"level1\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_exit_to_root_scope() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n        stack.enter_scope(4, 2, Some(\"web\".to_string()));\n        stack.enter_scope(6, 3, Some(\"config\".to_string()));\n\n        stack.exit_to_scope(0);\n\n        verify_scope_state(&stack, 1, \"\");\n        assert_eq!(stack.current_indent(), 0);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_add_key_to_root_scope() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        let result = stack.add_key(\"root_key\", 1);\n        assert!(result.is_ok(), \"Should successfully add key to root scope\");\n\n        verify_key_exists(&stack, \"root_key\", true);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_add_key_to_nested_scope() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n        stack.enter_scope(4, 2, Some(\"web\".to_string()));\n\n        let result = stack.add_key(\"host\", 3);\n        assert!(result.is_ok());\n\n        verify_key_exists(&stack, \"host\", true);\n        verify_scope_state(&stack, 3, \"services.web\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_add_multiple_keys_to_scope() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        stack.add_key(\"first\", 1).unwrap();\n        stack.add_key(\"second\", 2).unwrap();\n        stack.add_key(\"third\", 3).unwrap();\n\n        verify_key_exists(&stack, \"first\", true);\n        verify_key_exists(&stack, \"second\", true);\n        verify_key_exists(&stack, \"third\", true);\n\n        assert_eq!(stack.current_scope_ref().key_count(), 3);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_duplicate_key_in_same_scope() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        stack.add_key(\"host\", 1).unwrap();\n\n        let result = stack.add_key(\"host\", 2);\n\n        assert!(result.is_err(), \"Should detect duplicate key in same scope\");\n\n        let error = result.unwrap_err();\n        assert_eq!(error.key, \"host\");\n        assert_eq!(error.first_line, 0); // Root scope starts at line 0\n        assert_eq!(error.duplicate_line, 2);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_duplicate_key_error_message() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n        stack.add_key(\"host\", 2).unwrap();\n\n        let result = stack.add_key(\"host\", 5);\n        let error = result.unwrap_err();\n\n        let message = error.message();\n        assert!(message.contains(\"duplicate key\"));\n        assert!(message.contains(\"host\"));\n        assert!(message.contains(\"services\"));\n        assert!(message.contains(\"Line 5\"));\n        assert!(message.contains(\"Line 1\")); // Scope started at line 1, not where key was added\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_same_key_different_scopes_allowed() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        // Add \"host\" to root scope\n        stack.add_key(\"host\", 1).unwrap();\n\n        // Enter nested scope\n        stack.enter_scope(2, 2, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n\n        // Same key should be allowed in different scope\n        let result = stack.add_key(\"host\", 3);\n        assert!(result.is_ok(), \"Same key should be allowed in different scopes\");\n\n        verify_key_exists(&stack, \"host\", true);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_same_key_sibling_scopes_allowed() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        // First sibling scope\n        stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"web\".to_string()));\n        stack.add_key(\"host\", 2).unwrap();\n        stack.add_key(\"port\", 3).unwrap();\n\n        // Exit to root and enter second sibling\n        stack.exit_to_scope(0);\n        stack.enter_scope(2, 5, Some(\"database\".to_string()));\n\n        // Same keys should be allowed in sibling scope\n        stack.add_key(\"host\", 6).unwrap();\n        stack.add_key(\"port\", 7).unwrap();\n\n        verify_key_exists(&stack, \"host\", true);\n        verify_key_exists(&stack, \"port\", true);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_scope_isolation_deep_nesting() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        // Create deeply nested scopes with same key at each level\n        stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"level1\".to_string()));\n        stack.add_key(\"value\", 2).unwrap();\n\n        stack.enter_scope(4, 3, Some(\"level2\".to_string()));\n        stack.add_key(\"value\", 4).unwrap();\n\n        stack.enter_scope(6, 5, Some(\"level3\".to_string()));\n        stack.add_key(\"value\", 6).unwrap();\n\n        stack.enter_scope(8, 7, Some(\"level4\".to_string()));\n        stack.add_key(\"value\", 8).unwrap();\n\n        // Each scope should have exactly one key\n        assert_eq!(stack.current_scope_ref().key_count(), 1);\n\n        // Duplicate in current scope should fail\n        assert!(stack.add_key(\"value\", 9).is_err());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_enter_sequence_scope_creates_isolated_scope() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 1);\n\n        assert!(stack.in_sequence_context());\n        assert_eq!(stack.current_indent(), 2);\n        assert_eq!(stack.depth(), 2); // Root + sequence scope\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_sequence_scope_isolation() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        // First sequence item\n        stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 1);\n        stack.add_key(\"name\", 2).unwrap();\n        stack.add_key(\"port\", 3).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(stack.current_scope_ref().key_count(), 2);\n\n        // Second sequence item (same indent, new scope)\n        stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 5);\n\n        // Keys should be cleared for new sequence item\n        assert_eq!(stack.current_scope_ref().key_count(), 0);\n        assert!(!stack.contains_key(\"name\"));\n        assert!(!stack.contains_key(\"port\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_sequence_scope_unique_item_ids() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 1);\n        let first_id = stack.current_scope_ref().sequence_item_id;\n\n        stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 3);\n        let second_id = stack.current_scope_ref().sequence_item_id;\n\n        stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 5);\n        let third_id = stack.current_scope_ref().sequence_item_id;\n\n        assert_eq!(first_id, Some(1));\n        assert_eq!(second_id, Some(2));\n        assert_eq!(third_id, Some(3));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_sequence_scope_allows_same_keys_across_items() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        // First item\n        stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 1);\n        stack.add_key(\"host\", 2).unwrap();\n        stack.add_key(\"port\", 3).unwrap();\n\n        // Second item - same keys should be OK\n        stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 5);\n        stack.add_key(\"host\", 6).unwrap();\n        stack.add_key(\"port\", 7).unwrap();\n\n        // Third item - again, same keys OK\n        stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 10);\n        stack.add_key(\"host\", 11).unwrap();\n        stack.add_key(\"port\", 12).unwrap();\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_sequence_scope_duplicate_within_item() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 1);\n        stack.add_key(\"name\", 2).unwrap();\n\n        // Duplicate within same sequence item should fail\n        let result = stack.add_key(\"name\", 3);\n        assert!(result.is_err());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_mixed_regular_and_sequence_scopes() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        // Regular scope\n        stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n        stack.add_key(\"web\", 2).unwrap();\n\n        // Sequence scope inside regular scope\n        stack.enter_sequence_scope(4, 3);\n        stack.add_key(\"name\", 4).unwrap();\n\n        // Exit sequence scope\n        stack.exit_to_scope(2);\n        assert!(!stack.in_sequence_context());\n\n        // Add to regular scope again\n        stack.add_key(\"database\", 5).unwrap();\n\n        assert!(stack.contains_key(\"web\"));\n        assert!(stack.contains_key(\"database\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_reset_clears_all_scopes() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"level1\".to_string()));\n        stack.enter_scope(4, 2, Some(\"level2\".to_string()));\n        stack.add_key(\"key1\", 3).unwrap();\n        stack.add_key(\"key2\", 4).unwrap();\n\n        assert_eq!(stack.depth(), 3);\n\n        stack.reset();\n\n        assert_eq!(stack.depth(), 1);\n        assert_eq!(stack.current_indent(), 0);\n        assert!(!stack.contains_key(\"key1\"));\n        assert!(!stack.contains_key(\"key2\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_contains_key_in_current_scope_only() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        // Add to root\n        stack.add_key(\"root_key\", 1).unwrap();\n\n        // Enter nested scope\n        stack.enter_scope(2, 2, Some(\"nested\".to_string()));\n\n        // Root key should not be in current scope\n        assert!(!stack.contains_key(\"root_key\"));\n\n        // But should exist in some scope\n        assert!(stack.contains_key_in_any_scope(\"root_key\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_contains_key_in_any_scope() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        stack.add_key(\"level0\", 1).unwrap();\n\n        stack.enter_scope(2, 2, Some(\"level1\".to_string()));\n        stack.add_key(\"level1_key\", 3).unwrap();\n\n        stack.enter_scope(4, 4, Some(\"level2\".to_string()));\n        stack.add_key(\"level2_key\", 5).unwrap();\n\n        // From deepest scope, check all levels\n        assert!(stack.contains_key_in_any_scope(\"level0\"));\n        assert!(stack.contains_key_in_any_scope(\"level1_key\"));\n        assert!(stack.contains_key_in_any_scope(\"level2_key\"));\n\n        // Current scope only\n        assert!(!stack.contains_key(\"level0\"));\n        assert!(!stack.contains_key(\"level1_key\"));\n        assert!(stack.contains_key(\"level2_key\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_get_scope_at_level() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"level2\".to_string()));\n        stack.enter_scope(4, 2, Some(\"level4\".to_string()));\n        stack.enter_scope(6, 3, Some(\"level6\".to_string()));\n\n        assert!(stack.get_scope_at_level(0).is_some());\n        assert!(stack.get_scope_at_level(2).is_some());\n        assert!(stack.get_scope_at_level(4).is_some());\n        assert!(stack.get_scope_at_level(6).is_some());\n        assert!(stack.get_scope_at_level(8).is_none());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_scope_display_implementation() {\n        let scope = Scope::new(2, 5, Some(\"parent\".to_string()));\n        let display = format!(\"{}\", scope);\n\n        assert!(display.contains(\"Scope\"));\n        assert!(display.contains(\"indent=2\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_scope_stack_display_implementation() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n        stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"test\".to_string()));\n\n        let display = format!(\"{}\", stack);\n\n        assert!(display.contains(\"ScopeStack\"));\n        assert!(display.contains(\"depth=\"));\n        assert!(display.contains(\"base_indent=2\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_exit_to_scope_creates_fallback_if_missing() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        // Manually manipulate to test edge case\n        stack.enter_scope(4, 1, Some(\"level4\".to_string()));\n\n        // Exit to level 2 (which doesn't exist)\n        stack.exit_to_scope(2);\n\n        // Should create a fallback scope\n        assert_eq!(stack.current_indent(), 2);\n        verify_scope_state(&stack, 2, \"\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_complex_yaml_structure_simulation() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        // Simulate:\n        // services:\n        //   web:\n        //     host: localhost\n        //     port: 8080\n        //   database:\n        //     host: db.example.com\n        //     port: 5432\n\n        stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n\n        // web section\n        stack.enter_scope(4, 2, Some(\"web\".to_string()));\n        stack.add_key(\"host\", 3).unwrap();\n        stack.add_key(\"port\", 4).unwrap();\n\n        // database section (sibling)\n        stack.exit_to_scope(2);\n        stack.enter_scope(4, 5, Some(\"database\".to_string()));\n\n        // Same keys should be OK in sibling scope\n        stack.add_key(\"host\", 6).unwrap();\n        stack.add_key(\"port\", 7).unwrap();\n\n        // Verify final state\n        verify_scope_state(&stack, 3, \"services.database\");\n        verify_key_exists(&stack, \"host\", true);\n        verify_key_exists(&stack, \"port\", true);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_sequence_within_mapping() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        // Simulate:\n        // items:\n        //   - name: item1\n        //   - name: item2\n\n        stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"items\".to_string()));\n\n        // First sequence item\n        stack.enter_sequence_scope(4, 2);\n        stack.add_key(\"name\", 3).unwrap();\n assert_eq!(stack.current_scope_ref().key_count(), 1);\n\n        // Both items should have \"name\" key without conflict\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_duplicate_key_preserves_scope_path() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n        stack.enter_scope(4, 2, Some(\"web\".to_string()));\n        stack.add_key(\"config\", 3).unwrap();\n\n        let error = stack.add_key(\"config\", 4).unwrap_err();\n\n        assert_eq!(error.scope_path, \"services.web\");\n        assert_eq!(error.key, \"config\");\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod scope_tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_scope_creation_with_all_parameters() {\n        let scope = Scope::new(4, 10, test_scope_add_key_returns_true_on_duplicate() {\n        let mut scope = Scope::new(0, 1, None);\n\n        scope.add_key(\"key\");\n        let result = scope.add_key(\"key\");\n\n        assert_eq!(result, true, \"Duplicate add should return true\");\n        assert_eq!(scope.key_count(), 1, \"Key count should not increase on duplicate\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn    assert_eq!(scope.key_count(), 3);\n        assert!(scope.contains_key(\"key1\"));\n        assert!(scope.contains_key(\"key2\"));\n        assert!(scope.contains_key(\"key3\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_scope_contains_key() {\n        let mut scope = Scope::new(0, 1, None);\n\n        assert!(!scope.contains_key(\"test\"));\n\n        scope.add_key(\"test\");\n\n        assert!(scope.contains_key(\"test\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_scope_clear_keys() {\n        let mut scope = Scope::new(0, 1, None);\n\n        scope.add_key(\"key1\");\n        scope.add_key(\"key2\");\n        scope.add_key(\"key3\");\n        assert_eq!(scope.key_count(), 3);\n\n        scope.clear_keys();\n\n        assert_eq!(scope.key_count(), 0);\n        assert!(!scope.contains_key(\"key1\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_scope_key_count() {\n        let mut scope = Scope::new(0, 1, None);\n\n        assert_eq!(scope.key_count(), 0);\n\n duplicate\n        assert_eq!(scope.key_count(), 2); // Should not change\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod duplicate_key_error_tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn application:\n        //   server:\n        //     host: localhost\n        //     port: 8080\n        //   database:\n        //     host: db.example.com\n        //     port: 5432\n        // structures\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parse_str_with_nested_yaml() {\n        let parser = BasicParser::new();\n        let yaml = r#\"\nservices:\n  web:\n    host: localhost\n    port: 8080\n    config:\n      debug: true\n      log_level: info\n  database:\n    host: db.example.com\n    port: 5432\n\"#;\n\n        let result = parser.parse_str(yaml);\n        assert!(result.is_success(), \"Should successfully parse nested YAML structure\");\n\n        let value = result.unwrap();\n        assert!(value.is_mapping());\n        let services = value.get(\"services\").unwrap();\n        assert!(services.is_mapping());\n    }\n\n    /// Test scope transitions on indent changes\n    #[test]\n    fn test_scope_transitions_on_indent_changes() {\n        let parser = BasicParser::new();\n        let yaml = r#\"\nlevel1:\n  level2:\n    level3:\n      key: value\n  back_to_level2:\n    another: value\n\"#;\n\n        let result = parser.parse_str(yaml);\n        assert!(result.is_success(), \"Should handle scope transitions on indent changes\");\n\n        let value = result.unwrap();\n        let level1 = value.get(\"level1\").unwrap().as_mapping().unwrap();\n\n        // Should have both level2 and back_to_level2 as keys\n        assert!(level1.contains_key(\"level2\"));\n        assert!(level1.contains_key(\"back_to_level2\"));\n    }\n\n    /// Test duplicate key detection in real YAML examples\n    #[test]\n    fn test_duplicate_key_detection_in_yaml() {\n        let parser = BasicParser::strict(); // strict mode detects duplicates\n        let yaml = r#\"\nconfig:\n  host: localhost\n  host: duplicate_host\n  port: 8080\n\"#;\n\n        let result = parser.validate_str(yaml);\n        assert!(!result.is_valid(), \"Should detect duplicate key 'host' in same scope\");\n\n        // Should have an error about duplicate 'host' key\n        let has_host_error = result.errors.iter().any(|e| e.message.contains(\"host\") || e.message.contains(\"duplicate\"));\n        assert!(has_host_error, \"Should have error mentioning 'host' or 'duplicate'\");\n    }\n\n    /// Test that valid YAML with same key in different scopes passes\n    #[test]\n    fn test_same_key_different_scopes_allowed() {\n        let parser = BasicParser::new();\n        let yaml = r#\"\nservices:\n  web:\n    host: localhost\n    port: 8080\n  database:\n    host: db.example.com\n    port: 5432\n\"#;\n\n        let result = parser.parse_str(yaml);\n        assert!(result.is_success(), \"Should allow same key 'host' in different scopes (web vs database)\");\n\n        let value = result.unwrap();\n        let services = value.get(\"services\").unwrap().as_mapping().unwrap();\n\n        let web = services.get(\"web\").unwrap().as_mapping().unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(web.get(\"host\").unwrap().as_str().unwrap(), \"localhost\");\n\n        let database = services.get(\"database\").unwrap().as_mapping().unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(database.get(\"host\").unwrap().as_str().unwrap(), \"db.example.com\");\n    }\n\n    /// Test duplicate detection in deeply nested structure\n    #[test]\n    fn test_duplicate_detection_deeply_nested() {\n        let parser = BasicParser::strict();\n        let yaml = r#\"\napplication:\n  config:\n    server:\n      settings:\n        timeout: 30\n        timeout: 60\n\"#;\n\n        let result = parser.validate_str(yaml);\n        assert!(!result.is_valid(), \"Should detect duplicate 'timeout' in deeply nested scope\");\n\n        let has_timeout_error = result.errors.iter().any(|e| e.message.contains(\"timeout\"));\n        assert!(has_timeout_error, \"Should have error mentioning 'timeout'\");\n    }\n\n    /// Test complex real-world YAML with multiple scope levels\n    #[test]\n    fn test_complex_real_world_yaml() {\n        let parser = BasicParser::new();\n        let yaml = r#\"\napplication:\n  name: MyApp\n  version: 1.0\n\n  server:\n    host: localhost\n    port: 8080\n    ssl:\n      enabled: true\n      cert_path: /path/to/cert\n\n  database:\n    primary:\n      host: db1.example.com\n      port: 5432\n    replica:\n      host: db2.example.com\n      port: 5432\n\nlogging:\n  level: info\n  file: app.log\n  format: json\n\"#;\n\n        let result = parser.parse_str(yaml);\n        assert!(result.is_success(), \"Should parse complex real-world YAML structure\");\n\n        let value = result.unwrap();\n\n        // Verify application.server.ssl exists\n        let app = value.get(\"application\").unwrap().as_mapping().unwrap();\n        let server = app.get(\"server\").unwrap().as_mapping().unwrap();\n        assert!(server.contains_key(\"ssl\"));\n\n        // Verify database has both primary and replica\n        let database = app.get(\"database\").unwrap().as_mapping().unwrap();\n        assert!(database.contains_key(\"primary\"));\n        assert!(database.contains_key(\"replica\"));\n    }\n\n    /// Test sequence with scope tracking\n    #[test]\n    fn test_sequence_scope_tracking() {\n        let parser = BasicParser::new();\n        let yaml = r#\"\nitems:\n  - name: item1\n    value: 100\n  - name: item2\n    value: 200\n  - name: item3\n    value: 300\n\"#;\n\n        let result = parser.parse_str(yaml);\n        assert!(result.is_success(), \"Should parse YAML with sequences\");\n\n        let value = result.unwrap();\n        let items = value.get(\"items\").unwrap().as_sequence().unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(items.len(), 3);\n    }\n\n    /// Test duplicate detection within sequence items\n    #[test]\n    fn test_duplicate_within_sequence_item() {\n        let parser = BasicParser::strict();\n        let yaml = r#\"\nitems:\n  - name: item1\n    value: 100\n    value: 200\n\"#;\n\n        let result = parser.validate_str(yaml);\n        // The sequence item should detect the duplicate 'value' key\n        assert!(!result.is_valid(), \"Should detect duplicate 'value' within sequence item\");\n    }\n\n    /// Test scope isolation between root keys\n    #[test]\n    fn test_root_scope_isolation() {\n        let parser = BasicParser::new();\n        let yaml = r#\"\nserver:\n  host: localhost\n  port: 8080\n\ndatabase:\n  host: db.example.com\n  port: 5432\n\ncache:\n  host: cache.example.com\n  port: 6379\n\"#;\n\n        let result = parser.parse_str(yaml);\n        assert!(result.is_success(), \"Should allow 'host' and 'port' in all three root scopes\");\n\n        let value = result.unwrap();\n\n        // Each should have independent host and port values\n        let server = value.get(\"server\").unwrap().as_mapping().unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(server.get(\"host\").unwrap().as_str().unwrap(), \"localhost\");\n        assert_eq!(server.get(\"port\").unwrap().as_i64().unwrap(), 8080);\n\n        let database = value.get(\"database\").unwrap().as_mapping().unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(database.get(\"host\").unwrap().as_str().unwrap(), \"db.example.com\");\n        assert_eq!(database.get(\"port\").unwrap().as_i64().unwrap(), 5432);\n\n        let cache = value.get(\"cache\").unwrap().as_mapping().unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(cache.get(\"host\").unwrap().as_str().unwrap(), \"cache.example.com\");\n        assert_eq!(cache.get(\"port\").unwrap().as_i64().unwrap(), 6379);\n    }\n\n    /// Test indent changes create proper scope transitions\n    #[test]\n    fn test_indent_creates_scope_hierarchy() {\n        let parser = BasicParser::new();\n        let yaml = r#\"\nroot:\n  level1_a:\n    level2_a:\n      key1: value1\n    level2_b:\n      key2: value2\n  level1_b:\n    level2_c:\n      key3: value3\n\"#;\n\n        let result = parser.parse_str(yaml);\n        assert!(result.is_success(), \"Should properly handle hierarchical scope structure\");\n\n        let value = result.unwrap();\n        let root = value.get(\"root\").unwrap().as_mapping().unwrap();\n\n        // Should have both level1_a and level1_b\n        assert!(root.contains_key(\"level1_a\"));\n        assert!(root.contains_key(\"level1_b\"));\n\n        // level1_a should have both level2_a and level2_b\n        let level1_a = root.get(\"level1_a\").unwrap().as_mapping().unwrap();\n        assert!(level1_a.contains_key(\"level2_a\"));\n        assert!(level1_a.contains_key(\"level2_b\"));\n    }\n\n    /// Test duplicate detection in sibling scopes\n    #[test]\n    fn test_duplicate_in_sibling_scope() {\n        let parser = BasicParser::strict();\n        let yaml = r#\"\nitems:\n  first:\n    name: Item1\n    description: First item\n  second:\n    name: Item2\n    name: DuplicateName\n\"#;\n\n        let result = parser.validate_str(yaml);\n        assert!(!result.is_valid(), \"Should detect duplicate 'name' in second scope\");\n    }\n\n    /// Test empty lines and comments don't affect scope tracking\n    #[test]\n    fn test_comments_and_empty_lines() {\n        let parser = BasicParser::new();\n        let yaml = r#\"\n# This is a comment\nservices:\n  # Web service configuration\n  web:\n    host: localhost\n    # Empty lines below\n\n    port: 8080\n\n  database:\n    host: db.example.com\n    port: 5432\n# End comment\n\"#;\n\n        let result = parser.parse_str(yaml);\n        assert!(result.is_success(), \"Should handle comments and empty lines correctly\");\n\n        let value = result.unwrap();\n        let services = value.get(\"services\").unwrap().as_mapping().unwrap();\n        assert!(services.contains_key(\"web\"));\n        assert!(services.contains_key(\"database\"));\n    }\n\n    /// Test flow style is handled correctly\n    #[test]\n    fn test_flow_style_mapping() {\n        let parser = BasicParser::new();\n        let yaml = r#\"\nservices: {web: {host: localhost, port: 8080}, database: {host: db.example.com}}\n\"#;\n\n        let result = parser.parse_str(yaml);\n        assert!(result.is_success(), \"Should handle flow style mapping\");\n\n        let value = result.unwrap();\n        assert!(value.get(\"services\").is_some());\n    }\n\n    /// Test multiline strings don't interfere with scope tracking\n    #[test]\n    fn test_multiline_strings() {\n        let parser = BasicParser::new();\n        let yaml = r#\"\nconfig:\n  description: |\n    This is a multiline\n    string that spans\n    multiple lines\n  another_key: value\n\"#;\n\n        let result = parser.parse_str(yaml);\n        assert!(result.is_success(), \"Should handle multiline strings\");\n\n        let value = result.unwrap();\n        let config = with scope tracking\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parse_file_with_scope_tracking() {\n        let parser = BasicParser::new();\n\n        // Create a temporary YAML file\n        let temp_dir = std::env::temp_dir();\n        let yaml_path = temp_dir.join(\"test-scope-tracking.yaml\");\n        let yaml_content = r#\"\napplication:\n  name: TestApp\n  config:\n    debug: true\n    log_level: info\n\"#;\n\n        std::fs::write(&yaml_path, yaml_content).expect(\"Failed to write temp file\");\n\n        let result = parser.parse_file(&yaml_path);\n        assert!(result.is_success(), test_validate_str_detects_duplicates() {\n        let parser = BasicParser::strict();\n        let yaml = r#\"\nconfig:\n  timeout: 30\n  timeout: 60\n  retries: 3\n  retries: 5\n\"#;\n\n        let result = parser.validate_str(yaml);\n        assert!(!result.is_valid(), \"Should detect multiple duplicate keys\");\n\n        // Should have at least 2 errors (one for timeout, one for retries)\n host: duplicate-value\n564\t    port: 8080\n565\t\"#;\n566\t\n567\t        // The parse_str itself may succeed (serde_yaml's behavior), but validation should detect the duplicate\n568\t        let
